segments.text: Lesson 43.

translations.zh-Hans: 第43课。

segments.text: Fully Insured.

translations.zh-Hans: 全额保险。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translations.zh-Hans: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: Who owned the pie dish, and why?

translations.zh-Hans: 馅饼盘属于谁？为什么？

segments.text: companies are normally willing to insure anything.

translations.zh-Hans: 保险公司通常愿意为任何东西承保。

segments.text: Insuring public or private property is a standard practice in most countries in the world.

translations.zh-Hans: 在世界大多数国家，为公共或私人财产投保都是惯例。

segments.text: If, however, you were holding an open area, It would be equally possible to insure yourself in the event of bad weather.

translations.zh-Hans: 不过，如果你要在露天举办活动，同样也可以为天气恶劣的情况投保。

segments.text: Needless to say, the bigger the risk an insurance company takes, the higher the premium you will have to pay.

translations.zh-Hans: 不用说，保险公司承担的风险越大，你要付的保险费就越高。

segments.text: It is not uncommon to hear that a shipping company has made a claim for the cost of salvaging a sunken ship, but the claim made by a local authority to recover.

translations.zh-Hans: 航运公司为打捞沉船的费用索赔并不少见，但某地方当局提出索赔，以收回

segments.text: The of salvaging a sunken pie dish must surely be unique.

translations.zh-Hans: 打捞一个沉没馅饼盘的费用，这恐怕是绝无仅有的。

segments.text: Admittedly, it was an unusual pie dish, for it was eighteen feet long and six feet wide.

translations.zh-Hans: 诚然，这是个不同寻常的馅饼盘，长18英尺、宽6英尺。

segments.text: It had been purchased by a local authority, so that an enormous pie could be baked for an annual fare.

translations.zh-Hans: 它由地方当局购买，用来为一年一度的集市烤制一个巨型馅饼。

segments.text: The pie committee decided that the best way to transport the dish would be by canal, so they insured it for the trip.

translations.zh-Hans: 馅饼委员会认为水路运输这个盘子最合适，因此为这段行程投了保。

segments.text: Shortly after it was launched, the pie committee went to a local inn to celebrate.

translations.zh-Hans: 盘子下水后不久，委员会成员到当地一家小酒馆庆祝去了。

segments.text: At the same time, a number of teenagers climbed onto the dish and held a little party of their own.

translations.zh-Hans: 与此同时，一群少年爬上盘子，举行了自己的小聚会。

segments.text: Dancing proved to be more than the dish could bear, for during the party it capsized and sank in seven feet of water.

translations.zh-Hans: 跳舞超出了盘子的承受能力，聚会期间它翻倒了，沉入7英尺深的水中。

segments.text: The pie committee telephoned a local garage owner who arrived in a recovery truck to salvage the pie dish.

translations.zh-Hans: 馅饼委员会给当地一位汽车修理厂老板打电话，他开着抢修车前来打捞。

segments.text: Shivering in their wet clothes, the teenagers looked on while three men dived repeatedly into the water to locate the dish.

translations.zh-Hans: 少年们穿着湿衣服冻得发抖，在一旁看着三个人反复潜入水中寻找盘子。

segments.text: They had little difficulty in finding it, but hauling it out of the water proved to be a serious problem.

translations.zh-Hans: 找到盘子并不难，但把它拖出水面却成了严重的问题。

segments.text: The sides of the dish were so smooth that it was almost impossible to attach hausers and chains to the rim without damaging it.

translations.zh-Hans: 盘壁十分光滑，几乎无法在不损坏它的情况下把粗缆和铁链系到边缘。

segments.text: Eventually, chains were fixed to one end of the dish and a powerful winch was put into operation.

translations.zh-Hans: 最后，铁链被固定在盘子的一端，一台强力绞车开始运转。

segments.text: The dish rose to the surface and was gently drawn towards the canal bank.

translations.zh-Hans: 盘子浮出水面，被轻轻拉向运河岸边。

segments.text: For one agonizing moment, the dish was perched precariously on the bank of the canal, but it suddenly overbalanced and slid back into the water.

translations.zh-Hans: 在令人揪心的一瞬间，盘子摇摇欲坠地搭在岸边，随后突然失去平衡，又滑回水中。

segments.text: The men were now obliged to try once more.

translations.zh-Hans: 人们只得再试一次。

segments.text: This time, they fixed heavy metal clamps to both sides of the dish so that they could fasten the chains.

translations.zh-Hans: 这一次，他们在盘子两侧装上沉重的金属夹，以便固定铁链。

segments.text: The dish now had to be lifted vertically because one edge was resting against the side of the canal.

translations.zh-Hans: 因为盘子的一边靠着运河壁，现在必须垂直把它吊起。

segments.text: The winch was again put into operation and one of the men started up the truck.

translations.zh-Hans: 绞车再次启动，其中一人也发动了卡车。

segments.text: Several minutes later, the dish was successfully hauled above the surface of the water.

translations.zh-Hans: 几分钟后，盘子终于被成功吊出水面。

segments.text: Water streamed in torrents over its sides with such force that it set up a huge wave in the canal.

translations.zh-Hans: 水从盘子两侧倾泻而下，力量之大在运河里掀起一股巨浪。

segments.text: There was danger that the wave would rebound off the other side of the bank and send the dish plunging into the water again.

translations.zh-Hans: 巨浪可能从对岸反弹回来，把盘子再次冲进水里。

segments.text: By working at tremendous speed, the men managed to get the dish onto dry land before the wave returned.

translations.zh-Hans: 人们飞快地工作，终于在巨浪返回之前把盘子弄到了干地上。
